---
title: "Multimodal digital trace data research using Screenomics and multi-agent systems"
subtitle: "... and some thoughts on the dynamics of meanings"
author:
  - name: "卓牧融 | Mu-Jung 'MJ' Cho"
    orcid: '0000-0001-5500-6336'
    email: 'mjcho@as.edu.tw'
    affiliations: 
      - name: 'RCHSS, Academia Sinica'
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: https://media1.tenor.com/m/TmIXaAyzuwwAAAAd/space-art-stars-cosmic.gif
    # /imgs/synthwave.gif # miami_nights1984-sentimental.jpeg 
    # data-background-size: 120%, auto
    # data-background-position: bottom
    data-background-opacity: "0.2"
format:
  revealjs:
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    theme: [default, synthwave84-reveal-1.scss]
    slide-number: c
    incremental: true
    slide-level: 2
    # smaller: true
    scrollable: true
execute:
  echo: true
occassion: "@ NTU Psychology Seminar"
date: last-modified
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
# minimal setup chunk
```

## Why All These Matters?

---

## A Tale of Scientific Evolution

When Biology Becomes Computable, Mind Becomes Modelable

* Cognition emerges from biological information processing.
* Multi‑scale biological models create mechanistic foundations for psychological theory.
* Integrated biological + digital‑trace data enables predictive models of behavior.
* Synthetic agents and personas become useful testbeds for psychological mechanisms.
* Psychology shifts from primarily correlational explanations toward mechanistic and intervention‑driven science.

---

# Human–AI Interaction for Understanding Online Public Opinion

---

## Real‑Time Assistance for Understanding Social Issues
### Research Team and Background

Interdisciplinary team at RCHSS, Academia Sinica:

* Mu‑Jung (MJ) Cho
* Chingching Chang
* Yuan Hsiao
* Hen‑Hsen Huang
* Yong‑Ju Chang

Project goal:

* Improve public understanding of social issues
* Facilitate more constructive public dialogue

Research foundation:

* The largest online public‑opinion database in Taiwan

---

## Roots of Social Controversy: Gaps in Understanding

Cognitive biases interacting with new media environments create **understanding gaps**.

Examples include:

* Cognitive dissonance and selective exposure
* Algorithmic filtering and echo chambers
* Fragmentation of information in digital media
* Diffusion and instability of epistemic authority

These dynamics make it increasingly difficult to form shared public understanding.

---

## Improving Issue Understanding Through Human–AI Interaction

Multimodal public‑opinion data × RAG × multi‑agent systems

Core components:

* Taiwan's most comprehensive online public‑opinion database
* Retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG) to improve information accessibility
* Multi‑agent collaboration to generate deeper synthesis
* Interactive framing that guides user reasoning

---

## Human‑Centered Multimodal Empirical Research

A comprehensive methodological approach:

From social science to human–computer interaction

Key directions:

* Rapid growth of multimodal data in social science
* Cross‑modal analysis (text, images, social interaction dynamics)
* Human–AI interaction as a tool to deepen issue understanding

---

## Multi‑Agent System Architecture

RAG × vector databases × collaborative agents

System workflow:

Drafting → Critiquing → Refining

Technical components:

* Vector database: multilingual‑e5 embeddings + ChromaDB
* RAG question‑answering agent
* Summarization agent (Gemini models)

System capabilities:

* Iterative refinement of responses
* Position extraction and argument mapping
* Video and multimodal content summarization

---

## Guided Human–AI Interaction: Perspective Spectrum and Q&A

System interface supports:

* Mapping diverse viewpoints across a spectrum
* Guided Q&A to identify key points of disagreement
* Structured exploration of complex issues

Goal:

* Help users understand multiple perspectives
* Reduce binary polarization
* Support more nuanced public discussion

---

## Human–AI Interaction for Issue Understanding

Key contribution:

The system helps users navigate complex public controversies by presenting structured perspectives and arguments.

Expected benefits:

* More informed public reasoning
* Reduced polarization
* Greater capacity for constructive civic dialogue

---

## A New Phase for Social Science

Behavior becomes measurable across multiple layers:

* Biological processes
* Cognitive dynamics
* Digital trace environments

This integration allows:

* Emotion and thought contagion to be modeled more precisely
* Media effects to be studied at multiple temporal and social scales
* Computational social science to connect with biological and cognitive models

The result may be a new phase of social science:

**A designable science of human behavior.**

---

## Thank You

Questions?

Mu‑Jung 'MJ' Cho

[mjcho@as.edu.tw](mailto:mjcho@as.edu.tw)
